steps.title: Wash around the sink with hot...
steps.instruction: Wash around the sink with hot tap water and lay brown paper towels on both sides of the sink, covering the work area.

steps.title: Using hot tap water, wash the...
steps.instruction: Using hot tap water, wash the plastic buckets, the three sieves, the graduated cylinder, and the pair of scissors.

steps.title: Cut the tops (leaves, branches, and...
steps.instruction: Cut the tops (leaves, branches, and main stem) off the infected plants. Wash the roots by dipping the soil in a plastic bucket full of water. Rinse the roots by dipping them in a second plastic bucket full of water until all the soil particles have been removed from the roots.

steps.title: Cut the clean roots with scissors...
steps.instruction: Cut the clean roots with scissors and remove the remaining main plant stem.

steps.title: Prepare a 10% bleach solution by...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 10% bleach solution by pouring 100 mL of bleach into the graduated cylinder. Add water to a final volume of 1,000 mL (before preparing the bleach solution, check the “Before Starting” section for more information).

steps.title: Place the roots of about one...
steps.instruction: Place the roots of about one plant into the clean blender or shaker. Cover the roots with 10% bleach, just enough to cover them entirely, which should take c. 350 mL to 1 L.

steps.title: Blend or shake the roots for 3 mins
steps.instruction: Blend or shake the roots for 3 mins.

steps.title: Meanwhile, place the 170-, 500-, and...
steps.instruction: Meanwhile, place the 170-, 500-, and 500-mesh sieves on the plastic bucket in the following order: the 500-mesh sieves at the bottom and the 170-mesh sieve at the top (eggs will be collected from the two 500-mesh sieves).

steps.title: Pour the blended or shaken roots...
steps.instruction: Pour the blended or shaken roots into the top sieve of the stack of sieves and rinse with tap water. Rinse until the white bleach has disappeared. Use the mallet to hit the sieves gently on their sides to prevent the sieve pores from clogging. Discard the plant material collected in the top sieve.

steps.title: Rinse the debris in the second...
steps.instruction: Rinse the debris in the second sieve (500-mesh sieve) until all bleach has disappeared, and gently gather the root-knot nematode eggs on one side of the sieve. Collect the eggs with tap water into a clean plastic beaker using a wash bottle.

steps.title: Gently rinse the fine debris in...
steps.instruction: Gently rinse the fine debris in the last 500-mesh sieve until all bleach has disappeared. Carefully gather the root-knot nematode eggs again on one side of this last sieve, so no eggs will be lost through the screen. Use the wash bottle to collect the eggs into the plastic beaker.

steps.title: Pass the water that was poured...
steps.instruction: Pass the water that was poured into the plastic bucket through the bottom 500-mesh sieve to collect any eggs that may have escaped. Gather the nematode eggs and collect them in a different plastic beaker. If this egg suspension is clean enough (no soil particles visible), then both egg suspensions can be poured together into the same beaker for further quantification.

steps.title: Repeat until all the root material has been processed
steps.instruction: Repeat until all the root material has been processed.
